Ferula is a plant that grows in the mountainous regions of Asia and is known for its strong, pungent smell. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its purported health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The bioactive constituents of Ferula include volatile oils such as asafoetida and ferulic acid, which are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Ferula is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as asafoetida powder, which is used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations.
